dual_intent_strategy.primary_intent: commercial — buyer evaluating the free AVS-certified training lesson bundled with every Curious Tails puppy
dual_intent_strategy.secondary_intent: informational — owner researching what AVS accreditation means and what first-lesson training covers
dual_intent_strategy.resolution: Explain the ACDT scheme and rewards-based method credibly (informational win, AVS itself ranks 1-2), then anchor the unique offer: the first lesson is included free with every puppy, timed inside the critical socialisation window.

meta_title: Free Puppy Training Lesson | AVS-Certified Trainer | Curious Tails
meta_description: Every Curious Tails puppy includes a free first training lesson with an AVS-Accredited Certified Dog Trainer: settling in, potty routine, rewards-based methods. WhatsApp us to book.
h1: Your Free First Dog Training Lesson, AVS-Certified

entity_definitions.definition: Curious Tails is an AVS licensed pet shop in Balestier, Singapore (licence AS24J00046) that includes a free first training lesson with an AVS-Accredited Certified Dog Trainer with every puppy.

The AVS-Accredited Certified Dog Trainer (ACDT) Scheme, launched 10 December 2022, is Singapore's national benchmark for dog-training competency. Accredited trainers hold certification from recognised international animal behaviour organisations and use rewards-based, science-backed methods.

serp_analysis.dominant_content_format: AVS government pages + training school service pages + listicles
serp_analysis.search_intent_confirmation: Mixed: owners looking for accredited trainers, and buyers validating the 'AVS certified' claim; forum threads ask 'who do you recommend from the AVS list'.

featured_snippet.dog training Singapore AVS certified: none — target paragraph capsule defining ACDT

serp_analysis.knowledge_panel: n/a for us; entity via LocalBusiness + Service schema

what a first lesson actually covers for a brand-new owner (settling, potty routine)

sourcing_transparency_gaps: schools rarely link the official ACDT list for verification
regulatory_compliance_gaps: PADT scheme discontinued 10 Dec 2024 — outdated claims still circulating

no trust signals near booking CTAs

factual_conflicts: "AVS-accredited under old PADT scheme" vs "ACDT is the only current scheme" → resolution: PADT discontinued 10 Dec 2024; only ACDT accreditation is current. Use ACDT terminology exactly.

faq.question: Is the training lesson really free?
faq.answer_direction: Yes; it is included in every puppy's $2,888 to $4,488 price, delivered by an AVS-Accredited Certified Dog Trainer, no add-on fee.

faq.question: When should the first lesson happen?
faq.answer_direction: In week one home, inside the 3-to-16-week socialisation window; we help you book it during the delivery walkthrough.

faq.question: What does the lesson cover?
faq.answer_direction: Settling in, the playpen and potty routine, and handling basics for the owner; the goal is a simple routine you can actually follow.

faq.question: What if my puppy is shy or overwhelmed?
faq.answer_direction: Rewards-based methods work at the puppy's pace; quality over quantity is the AVS-endorsed principle, and the trainer adapts to your puppy.

faq.question: How much do further lessons cost?
faq.answer_direction: That is between you and whichever ACDT trainer you continue with; packages in Singapore vary widely. Our care support on WhatsApp stays free either way.

faq.question: How do I verify a trainer is AVS-accredited?
faq.answer_direction: Check the official ACDT list on the AVS website; accreditation requires certification from recognised international behaviour organisations.
